text.title: vui-conversation-design Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the vui-conversation-design methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Dialog MUST be a state machine. Every state MUST list allowed intents, transitions, and prompts.

rationale.source: Cathy Pearl — Designing Voice User Interfaces

rule.rationale: Hidden control flow causes loops and orphan states; explicit machine catches them at design time.

rule.statement: Reprompts MUST cap at 3 per state. Fourth miss MUST escalate (fallback or human).
Every spoken prompt MUST be ≤12 words. Multi-clause prompts MUST be split.
rule.rationale: Listeners cannot retain >12 words; long prompts cause cognitive overload.

rule.statement: Voice script and screen prompt MUST be separate fields. Reusing screen content as voice content is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Screen content has visual scan affordances voice lacks; voice scripts need natural cadence.

rule.statement: Conversation MUST be tested in ≥3 noise conditions (quiet, ambient noise, multiple speakers). Quiet-only testing is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Real environments degrade ASR; testing only in quiet inflates accuracy estimates.

rule.statement: Every state MUST define explicit handlers for no-match (silence) and ambiguous (multiple intents matched) inputs.
rule.rationale: Default fallthrough on no-match or ambiguous yields infinite loops or wrong actions.
